sources.notes: Seven loci reach genome-wide significance in combined stage 1+2 schizophrenia analysis. New loci: (1) MIR137 at 1p21.3 (rs1625579, P=1.6e-11); (2) 10q24.32-q24.33 — two independent signals, region contains multiple genes; (3) MMP16 at 8q21.3 (rs7004633, matrix metallopeptidase 16 named explicitly); (4) CSMD1 at 8p23.2 (rs10503253, CUB and Sushi multiple domains 1 named explicitly); (5) 2q32.3 near non-coding RNA PCGEM1 — no protein-coding gene named. Previously reported loci: (6) MHC 6p21.32-p22.1; (7) TCF4 at 18q21.2 (text names TCF4 as 'encoding transcription factor 4'). NRGN at 11q24.2: named in stage-1 results as a known locus; new signal is uncorrelated with the NRGN variant but the locus is in the results. Joint schizophrenia+bipolar analysis: CACNA1C (P=7.0e-9), ANK3 (P=2.5e-8), ITIH3-ITIH4 region (P=7.8e-9); ITIH4 coding variant p.Pro698Thr in perfect LD with top SNP. MAD1L1: nearly genome-wide significant independent conditional signal at 10q24.32 (P=5.06e-10), named explicitly as 'mitotic arrest deficient-like 1'. MIR137 predicted targets with GWAS support — TCF4 confirmed by LHA Tsai personal communication (TCF4 protein expression changes with MIR137 OE/KD). ZNF804A and DRD2: mentioned as previously implicated loci present in stage-1 data for the joint BD+SCZ context. Excluded: PCGEM1 (non-coding RNA, locus not named by gene), hundreds of MIR137 predicted targets mentioned only in enrichment analysis (not individual GWAS results), NRG1/DTNBP1/DISC1/DAOA (background literature only).
